draft:
RE: Recommendation for Ms. Zina Marokova


Dear Sir or Madam,


I am writing this letter to provide my strongest recommendation for Ms. Zina Marokova, who has proven herself to be an extraordinary leader in the consumer goods industry, particularly in the field of digital marketing transformation. 


I am Amir Kruger, currently serving as Global Vice President and Chief Digital Officer of the Baby Care division at Zenith Industries (Z&I). With over 18 years of experience leading strategy, digital transformation, and product management across global markets, I have had the opportunity to work with many exceptional professionals. Among them, Zina stands out as one of the most innovative and impactful individuals I've had the pleasure of managing.


I managed Zina for over four years in Zurich, Switzerland, where we both worked on SnuggleBaby's Global Strategy team. In this capacity, I closely observed her exceptional strategic thinking and leadership skills. Zina consistently demonstrated the ability to excel in high-pressure environments and deliver outstanding results. 


Zina is an incredibly intelligent, accomplished, and uniquely insightful leader. She holds degrees from two prestigious Ivy League institutions: Colgate University (undergraduate) and the Sloan School of Management (MBA). Her analytical prowess enables her to deduce strategic business needs from limited data, making her an invaluable asset in navigating complex marketing landscapes. She is highly inquisitive and approaches challenges with a problem-solving mindset that inspires those around her. Her ability to lead and drive teams toward achieving ambitious goals is truly exceptional. 


At Z&I, Zina played a pivotal role in transforming the digital marketing landscape for our SnuggleBaby global marketing team. She led the global strategy for SnuggleBaby-owned digital products, shifting our marketing model from relying on third-party data to first-party data-driven approaches. This included overseeing critical digital assets like the SnuggleBaby Rewards/Club app, loyalty programs, SnuggleBaby.com, and our email engagement program. Her strategic vision covered over 60 markets, reaching 150 million consumers annually, while she directly managed our top 10 markets, including the U.S. and Canada. 


Zina's expertise in introducing agile product methodologies to Z&I significantly optimized our product delivery processes. She led a cross-functional team of over 50 product and technical experts across four locations, implementing biweekly product roadmaps and aligning quarterly outcomes directly with Vice Presidents in key regions. Additionally, Zina introduced performance-based marketing to Z&I, effectively managing a multimillion-dollar media budget to drive customer acquisition. Her efforts led to a remarkable 65% acquisition rate among new moms within six months—up from 16%—while simultaneously reducing the cost per acquisition.


Zina's impact on Z&I’s global marketing strategy not only saved the company millions of dollars in marketing expenses but also attracted the attention of Z&I’s CEO and Board of Directors. The success of her digital transformation initiatives led to the reapplication of her strategies across other key Z&I brands, such as Blades and Sparkle, in over 60 geographies. 


Beyond her work at Z&I, I have also witnessed Zina’s impact in the consumer goods industry as an advisor to Learn & Play, where she served as Chief Marketing Officer. Zina transformed Learn & Play’s marketing strategy, driving their online sales from zero to millions of dollars within just a couple of years. Moreover, she played a critical role in incubating a breakthrough brand with significant potential to scale into a $100 million venture-backed consumer brand. 


In recognition of her outstanding contributions, Zina was awarded the Z&I CEO Award—an honor reserved for the top 1% of employees globally. This prestigious award reflects her exceptional ability to drive strategic initiatives that deliver substantial business impact. 


Zina Marokova is truly an extraordinary talent whose innovative contributions have left a lasting legacy at Z&I and beyond. Her proven expertise in digital marketing and consumer engagement positions her as a leader of extraordinary ability in her field. 


Yours sincerely,



Amir Kruger Global Vice President & Chief Digital Officer, Baby Care Zenith Industries
final:
Dear Sir or Madam,

	My name is Amir Kruger, Vice President and Chief Digital Officer of Global SnuggleBaby at Zenith Industries (Z&I), and I am pleased to provide this letter as a recommendation for Ms. Zina Marokova's visa petition. Ms. Marokova has proven herself to be an extraordinary leader in the consumer goods industry with deep expertise in digital strategy and digital marketing transformation for consumer goods and services. 

	As the Global Vice President of Growth, Transformation, and Digital for SnuggleBaby (Z&I Baby) for the past 6 years, I lead the end-to-end digital ecosystem, encompassing everything from digital product innovation and e-commerce growth to performance marketing and new venture development. Throughout my 19-year tenure at Z&I, I have driven transformative impact across multiple global markets, including the USA, Switzerland, Singapore, and Israel, managing complex digital initiatives and building high-performance teams. Prior to my current role, I served as Senior Brand Director and Head of Growth & Digital Transformation for Global SnuggleBaby in Zurich, where I led a multifunctional team of over 50 professionals and established a global CRM and loyalty program serving more than 10 million consumers worldwide. This extensive experience leading digital transformation at one of the world's largest consumer goods companies puts me in a unique position to evaluate talent in this space.
	During our time working together for over four years on SnuggleBaby's Global Strategy team in Zurich, Switzerland, I had the opportunity to closely observe Ms. Marokova's strategic thinking and leadership skills. She consistently demonstrated the ability to excel in high-pressure environments and deliver outstanding results.

	At Z&I, Ms. Marokova played a pivotal role in revolutionizing the digital marketing framework for the SnuggleBaby global marketing team. She spearheaded the global strategy for SnuggleBaby-owned digital products, transitioning the marketing model from a reliance on third-party data to a more robust, first-party data-driven approach. Her leadership extended to overseeing key digital assets, including the SnuggleBaby Rewards/Club app, loyalty programs, SnuggleBaby.com, and the email engagement program. With her strategic insights, she managed to cover over 60 markets, impacting 150 million consumers annually, and specifically drove initiatives in the top 10 markets, such as the U.S. and Canada.

	Ms. Marokova's tenure at Z&I was marked by several groundbreaking initiatives that set new benchmarks in the industry. By introducing agile product methodologies, she optimized product delivery processes, leading a diverse cross-functional team of more than 50 product and technical experts across four locations. Her implementation of biweekly product roadmaps and alignment of quarterly outcomes with Vice Presidents in key regions showcased her ability to seamlessly integrate strategic objectives with operational execution. Additionally, Ms. Marokova pioneered performance-based marketing at Z&I, efficiently managing a multimillion-dollar media budget to boost customer acquisition. Her efforts resulted in a significant increase in the acquisition rate among new moms from 16% to 65% within just six months, while simultaneously reducing the cost per acquisition.

	The innovative strategies that Ms. Marokova developed not only transformed SnuggleBaby’s marketing landscape but also generated substantial financial savings for Z&I, cutting millions in marketing expenses. Her work laid the groundwork for a new precedent in digital marketing for a billion-dollar brand within an $80 billion corporation. The comprehensive strategy she created combined consumer data gathering through content marketing and loyalty programs, which was then leveraged to enhance sales growth through efficient growth and retention marketing. This end-to-end strategy was a pioneering endeavor, encompassing millions of consumers across more than 60 markets and driving billions in sales. Its success led to the reapplication of her strategies across other major Z&I brands like Blades and Sparkle, underscoring the innovative impact of her contributions to the field.

	Ms. Marokova's transformative work at Z&I earned her significant recognition within the company. In 2018, she was honored with the prestigious Z&I CEO Award. This award is given annually to employees who demonstrate outstanding impact and sustained excellence over the fiscal year. Her achievements at Z&I also earned her several "Power of You" awards, acknowledging her remarkable contributions to the company's success. These accolades highlight Ms. Marokova's ability to deliver significant improvements and her consistent dedication to driving excellence in her role.

	Currently, Ms. Marokova holds pivotal roles at Learn & Play, serving as Chief Marketing Officer and General Manager of Online Sales. Since joining the company in June 2022, she has been instrumental in completely transforming Learn & Play's marketing strategy and driving online sales from the ground up to impressive multimillion-dollar figures within just a few years. Her contributions have not only revitalized the brand but have also positioned it for significant future growth.

	In her role as CMO, Ms. Marokova is responsible for overseeing marketing strategies and execution across Learn & Play's extensive reach of 35+ markets. Her leadership capacity expanded even further in January 2025 when she assumed the additional role of North America Sales Lead. This new responsibility places her in charge of managing all U.S. sales channels, where she now oversees 80% of the company’s revenue and ensures profitability for more than half of the business. Her capability to manage such a large portion of the business underscores her vital role in Learn & Play’s ongoing success.

	Beyond her current responsibilities, Ms. Marokova is at the forefront of launching a new brand, KinderLight. This initiative carries the potential to evolve into a $100 million venture-backed enterprise. She is actively engaged in fundraising discussions, aiming to secure $2 million in venture capital funding. Should her efforts prove successful, Ms. Marokova will become CEO and co-founder of KinderLight, reflecting her ongoing dedication to driving growth and innovation in the children's toy industry. Her work is not only impressive but also crucial to Learn & Play’s strategic vision and expansion goals.

	In conclusion, Ms. Marokova's remarkable career trajectory and proven expertise in digital strategy and marketing make her an invaluable asset in any organization she is part of. Her capacity to drive innovation, coupled with her strategic vision, has resulted in substantial growth and transformation for the companies she has served. Ms. Marokova continues to make significant strides in the consumer goods industry, and her leadership roles at both Z&I and Learn & Play highlight her ability to deliver impactful results. It's clear she will continue to be a pivotal force in developing innovative business strategies and spearheading transformational change.

Yours sincerely,



Amir Kruger Global Vice President & Chief Digital Officer, Baby Care Zenith Industries [insert contact information]
